title: build tag remote still requires dependencies like btrfs to be installed.
body:
### Issue Description

```bash
go install -tags "remote" ./cmd/...
# github.com/containers/storage/drivers/btrfs
vendor/github.com/containers/storage/drivers/btrfs/version.go:6:10: fatal error: btrfs/version.h: No such file or directory
    6 | #include <btrfs/version.h>
      |          ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
compilation terminated.
```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

go get -u github.com/containers/podman@latest

### Describe the results you received

see the above

### Describe the results you expected

expected to be able to compile my application without needed to install btrfs dependencies.

### podman info output

```yaml
github.com/containers/podman/v5 v5.5.2
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

_No response_

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body:
you need to use the `exclude_graphdriver_btrfs` build tag as well, we also use `containers_image_openpgp` for remote builds.

The way the code is structured and how go imports work it would be rather difficult to get rid of the github.com/containers/storage import on remote which causes all the drivers to get dragged in.
thanks, mostly this is a documentation issue/discovery issue I guess. Never saw those tags mentioned and i only stumbled across the remote one rarely.
@james-lawrence mind submitting a fix to the docs?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
